document.source_title: Turkey to buy 100 one-way explosive naval drones for swarm attacks
document.author: Cem Devrim Yaylali
document.summary: Turkey is set to procure 100 expendable unmanned surface vessels for its navy, a decision made during a February meeting of the Defense Industry Executive Committee. The procurement will be overseen by the Secretariat of Defense Industries, with three companies involved: Aselsan, STM, and Havelsan. These vessels are designed for precision strike missions and will operate in swarms, enhancing Turkey's naval capabilities.
key_takeaways: Turkey to buy 100 unmanned surface vessels for its navy.
Decision made by the Defense Industry Executive Committee in February.
Three companies involved: Aselsan, STM, and Havelsan.
Vessels designed for precision strike missions against maritime targets.
Swarm architecture allows cooperative operation and real-time data sharing.
Aselsan's Tufan and STM's Yaktu unveiled at SAHA Expo 2026.
document.why_it_matters: This procurement marks a significant enhancement of Turkey's naval capabilities, allowing for advanced operational strategies through the use of unmanned systems and swarm tactics, which are increasingly relevant in modern warfare.

claims.claim_text: Turkey is moving to buy 100 expendable unmanned surface vessels for its navy.
claims.evidence_text: Turkey is moving to buy 100 expendable unmanned surface vessels for its navy, Defense News learned during the SAHA Expo 2026 defense exhibition this month.

claims.claim_text: The decision to procure the systems was made during the February meeting of the Defense Industry Executive Committee.
claims.evidence_text: The decision to procure the systems was made during the February meeting of the Defense Industry Executive Committee, the highest decision-making body in Turkey’s defense procurement and industrial policy.

claims.claim_text: The 100 units are divided among three companies: Aselsan, STM, and Havelsan.
claims.evidence_text: The 100 units are divided as 40 for Aselsan, 32 for STM, and 32 for Havelsan.

entities.name: Aselsan
entities.description: A Turkish defense company involved in producing unmanned surface vessels.
entities.evidence_text: One of the three providers is Aselsan, which has teamed up with Ares Shipyard.

entities.name: STM
entities.description: A Turkish defense company cooperating with Yonca Shipyard to produce unmanned surface vessels.
entities.evidence_text: The second provider is STM, which is cooperating with Yonca Shipyard.

entities.name: Havelsan
entities.description: A Turkish defense company working with Sefine Shipyard on unmanned surface vessels.
entities.evidence_text: The third producer is Havelsan, which is working with Sefine Shipyard.
